{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638年}} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal Image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638th year }} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal Image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:11]] [[ja:明壽院]][[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:明壽院]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:明壽院]][[Category:11]][[sn:明壽院]][[Category:11]] [[sn:明壽院]][[Category:11]][[sn:明壽院]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:明壽院]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:明壽院]][[Category:11]] [[sn:明壽院]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|12=draft}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|12=draft}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|12=draft}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|12=draft}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|12=draft}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myoju-in]] [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}}

It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myoju-in]] [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}}

{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院|12=draft|12=draft}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|12=draft|12=draft}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山|12=draft|12=draft}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|12=draft|12=draft}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|12=draft|12=draft}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638th year|12=draft|12=draft}} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂|12=draft|12=draft}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛|12=draft|12=draft}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|12=draft|12=draft}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|12=draft|12=draft}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|12=draft|12=draft}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myoju-in]] [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}}

This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|12=draft|12=draft}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|12=draft|12=draft}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myoju-in]] [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂|12=draft|12=draft}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛|12=draft|12=draft}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|12=draft|12=draft}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|12=draft|12=draft}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myoju-in]] [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: 15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:明壽院 up to rev 101308058
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019年11月}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 明壽院 |画像 = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] |画像説明 = |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]桝屋町614 |位置 = |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 56|緯度秒 = 33.1 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 45|経度秒 = 37.7 |山号 = 光圓山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗醍醐派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[青面金剛|大青面金剛]] |創建年 = [[1638年]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 伏見庚申堂 |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |地図2 = }} '''明壽院'''（みょうじゅいん、明寿院）は、'''伏見庚申堂'''（ふしみ こうしんどう）とも呼ばれ[[庚申信仰]]の寺院。山号は'''光圓山'''（こうえんざん）という。
[[真言宗醍醐派]]、本尊は[[青面金剛|大青面金剛]]（だいしょうめん こんごう、庚申さん）を安置する。本尊は婦人病、下の病、夫婦和合の信仰集める。
== 歴史 == 1638年、開山。当初は、境内北に隣接する公園一帯を含め、大本堂、諸堂が建ち並んでいた。1698年、第113代・東山天皇の御願所になる。1868-1912年、明治期中期、本堂など破却される。その際に境内は現在の規模まで縮小された。
== 所在地 == [[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]桝屋町614
== 本尊 == 本堂に本尊の大青面金剛が安置されている。秘仏であり、厨子内に納められている。 60年に一度、庚申（かのえさる）の年にのみ開帳される。伝屍病鬼とも呼ばれる、伝死鬼（結核性・伝染性の病気）の難を除く。伝染病は見えない虫が媒介すると考えられてきたので、三尸の虫（さんしのむし、人の体内に棲む3匹の虫）を退治すると考えられ、庚申と習合した。古来、一切の病魔悪鬼を除き、一切の願いを成就させる修法がなされてきた。当寺では、毎日「青面金剛法」が修法されている。御前立がある。
眷属として4体の四大夜叉、2体の二大童子、[[三猿]]（見ざる、言わざる、聞かざる）、脇待、鎌倉時代作の[[不動明王]]、[[空海|弘法大師]]、東山天皇天牌を祀る。
== 庚申とは == 「[[十干]]（じっかん）」、甲・乙・丙・丁・戊・己・庚・辛・壬・癸）、「十二支（じゅうにし）」、子・丑・寅・卯・辰・巳・午・未・申・酉・戌・亥の組み合わせによる。組み合わせは60通りあり、60日に一度めぐる。
「庚申」の日の夜、人々は寝ずに一夜を明かす「庚申待ち」を行う。人の腹中には「三尸（さんし）の虫」が棲むという。庚申の日の夜、人々が寝静まると、虫は人の体から這い出すという。虫は、人が犯した悪事を天帝に告げ、それによって人の寿命が決まるとされた。
このため、人々は虫が抜け出さないように寝ずに過ごした。庚申待ちにより、三尸の虫を除き、息災延命、心身清浄を祈願した。
== お守り ==
=== くくり猿 === 猿が手足を括られ動けない姿を表している。
猿は、人の心中にある欲望をたとえている。欲望は、あたかも猿が走り回るように心中で動きまわる。努力を怠ろうとし、悪行を行う。願いを叶えようと努力すると妨げようとする。
これらの欲望を括り、庚申により制御してもらう。くくり猿の体内には、本尊・大青面金剛の御札が納められ、開眼の秘法により魂が込められている。
== 年間行事 == * こんにゃく封じ大祈願会（万病平癒、関節痛、腰痛封じのこんにゃく祈祷を厳修する。）（1月7日） * 星祭（立春開運厄除祈祷、柴燈大護摩、火渡り修行。）（2月11日） * 春季彼岸会中日法要（春分の日） * 精霊迎鐘（お盆） * 施餓鬼供養会（8月16日） * 秋季彼岸会中日法要（秋分の日） * 女人守護祈願会（女人幸福、女人守護の祈祷会を厳修する。）（10月第3日曜日） * 東山天皇忌・納骨堂総廻向（12月17日） * 除夜の鐘（12月31日） * 先祖・[[水子供養]]、庚申護摩（毎月7日） * 祈祷会（庚申の日）
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{デフォルトソート:みようしゆいん}} [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:庚申信仰]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[明壽院]] to [[Myoju-in]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019年11月}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 明壽院 |画像 = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] |画像説明 = |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]桝屋町614 |位置 = |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 56|緯度秒 = 33.1 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 45|経度秒 = 37.7 |山号 = 光圓山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗醍醐派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[青面金剛|大青面金剛]] |創建年 = [[1638年]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = 伏見庚申堂 |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |地図2 = }} '''明壽院'''（みょうじゅいん、明寿院）は、'''伏見庚申堂'''（ふしみ こうしんどう）とも呼ばれ[[庚申信仰]]の寺院。山号は'''光圓山'''（こうえんざん）という。
[[真言宗醍醐派]]、本尊は[[青面金剛|大青面金剛]]（だいしょうめん こんごう、庚申さん）を安置する。本尊は婦人病、下の病、夫婦和合の信仰集める。
== 歴史 == 1638年、開山。当初は、境内北に隣接する公園一帯を含め、大本堂、諸堂が建ち並んでいた。1698年、第113代・東山天皇の御願所になる。1868-1912年、明治期中期、本堂など破却される。その際に境内は現在の規模まで縮小された。
== 所在地 == [[京都府]][[京都市]][[伏見区]]桝屋町614
== 本尊 == 本堂に本尊の大青面金剛が安置されている。秘仏であり、厨子内に納められている。 60年に一度、庚申（かのえさる）の年にのみ開帳される。伝屍病鬼とも呼ばれる、伝死鬼（結核性・伝染性の病気）の難を除く。伝染病は見えない虫が媒介すると考えられてきたので、三尸の虫（さんしのむし、人の体内に棲む3匹の虫）を退治すると考えられ、庚申と習合した。古来、一切の病魔悪鬼を除き、一切の願いを成就させる修法がなされてきた。当寺では、毎日「青面金剛法」が修法されている。御前立がある。
眷属として4体の四大夜叉、2体の二大童子、[[三猿]]（見ざる、言わざる、聞かざる）、脇待、鎌倉時代作の[[不動明王]]、[[空海|弘法大師]]、東山天皇天牌を祀る。
== 庚申とは == 「[[十干]]（じっかん）」、甲・乙・丙・丁・戊・己・庚・辛・壬・癸）、「十二支（じゅうにし）」、子・丑・寅・卯・辰・巳・午・未・申・酉・戌・亥の組み合わせによる。組み合わせは60通りあり、60日に一度めぐる。
「庚申」の日の夜、人々は寝ずに一夜を明かす「庚申待ち」を行う。人の腹中には「三尸（さんし）の虫」が棲むという。庚申の日の夜、人々が寝静まると、虫は人の体から這い出すという。虫は、人が犯した悪事を天帝に告げ、それによって人の寿命が決まるとされた。
このため、人々は虫が抜け出さないように寝ずに過ごした。庚申待ちにより、三尸の虫を除き、息災延命、心身清浄を祈願した。
== お守り ==
=== くくり猿 === 猿が手足を括られ動けない姿を表している。
猿は、人の心中にある欲望をたとえている。欲望は、あたかも猿が走り回るように心中で動きまわる。努力を怠ろうとし、悪行を行う。願いを叶えようと努力すると妨げようとする。
これらの欲望を括り、庚申により制御してもらう。くくり猿の体内には、本尊・大青面金剛の御札が納められ、開眼の秘法により魂が込められている。
== 年間行事 == * こんにゃく封じ大祈願会（万病平癒、関節痛、腰痛封じのこんにゃく祈祷を厳修する。）（1月7日） * 星祭（立春開運厄除祈祷、柴燈大護摩、火渡り修行。）（2月11日） * 春季彼岸会中日法要（春分の日） * 精霊迎鐘（お盆） * 施餓鬼供養会（8月16日） * 秋季彼岸会中日法要（秋分の日） * 女人守護祈願会（女人幸福、女人守護の祈祷会を厳修する。）（10月第3日曜日） * 東山天皇忌・納骨堂総廻向（12月17日） * 除夜の鐘（12月31日） * 先祖・[[水子供養]]、庚申護摩（毎月7日） * 祈祷会（庚申の日）
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{デフォルトソート:みようしゆいん}} [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:庚申信仰]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]]

{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638th year}} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}}]] [[Category:ill|Temples of Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派の寺院}}]] [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:真言宗醍醐派の寺院]] [[Category:Kōshin]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Myoju-in]] [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Myoju-in]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|明壽院|version=101308058|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638th year}} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō}}

Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|12=draft}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|12=draft}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|12=draft}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|12=draft}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|12=draft}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} ]] }} {{translated page|ja|明壽院|version=101308058|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}} [[sn:Myoju-in]] }} [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}}

{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院|qq=draft}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|qq=draft}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山|qq=draft}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638th year|qq=draft}} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂|qq=draft}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛|qq=draft}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|qq=draft}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|qq=draft}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|qq=draft}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|qq=draft}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|qq=draft}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|qq=draft}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|qq=draft}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{citation needed|みょうじゅいん}} ]] }} {{translated page|ja|明壽院|version=101308058|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}} [[sn:Myoju-in]] }} [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] {{DEFAULTSORT:明壽院}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Myoju-in}}

{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoju-in|qq=}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Ward|qq=}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōenzan|qq=}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|qq=}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|qq=}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1638|qq=}} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Kōshin-dō|qq=}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Bishamondō|qq=}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushin Faith|qq=}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|qq=}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Seimen Kongō|qq=}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Ward|qq=}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushin|qq=}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Monkeys|qq=}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Higashiyama|qq=}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkan|qq=}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnishi|qq=}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] }} {{translated page|ja|明壽院|version=101308058|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}} [[sn:Myoju-in]] }} [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]]

Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] }} {{translated page|ja|明壽院|version=101308058|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}} [[sn:Myoju-in]] }} [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]]

{{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoju-in|qq=}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Ward|qq=}} Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōenzan|qq=}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|qq=}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|qq=}} | 創建年 = {{ill|1638|ja|1638年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1638|qq=}} | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Kōshin-dō|qq=}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Bishamondō|qq=}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with {{ill|Koushin Faith|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushin Faith|qq=}}. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|qq=}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Seimen Kongō|qq=}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}} {{ill|Fushimi Ward|ja|伏見区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Ward|qq=}} Masuya-cho 614 == Principal image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushin|qq=}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the {{ill|Three Monkeys|ja|三猿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Monkeys|qq=}} (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, and a tablet dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|ja|東山天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Higashiyama|qq=}}. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the {{ill|Tenkan|ja|十干|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkan|qq=}} (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the {{ill|Jūnishi|ja|十二支|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnishi|qq=}} (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] }} {{translated page|ja|明壽院|version=101308058|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}} [[sn:Myoju-in]] }} [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]]

By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. {{出典の明記|date=November 2019}} {{infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Myoju-in|ja|明壽院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoju-in|qq=}} | native_name = Myoju-in | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Myoju-in, Fushimi.jpg|300px]] | image_description = | address = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi Ward]] Masuya-cho 614 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 33.1 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 37.7 | 山号 = {{ill|Kōenzan|ja|光圓山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōenzan|qq=}} | 院号 = | 宗旨 = | 宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|qq=}} | 寺格 = | 本尊 = {{ill|Seimen Kongō|ja|青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimen Kongō|qq=}} | 創建年 = [[:en:1638|1638]] | 開山 = | 開基 = | 中興年 = | 中興 = | 正式名 = | 別称 = {{ill|Fushimi Kōshin-dō|ja|伏見Kōshin-dō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Kōshin-dō|qq=}}
{{ill|Yamamura Bishamondō|ja|山村毘沙門堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Bishamondō|qq=}} | 札所等 = | 文化財 = | 公式HP = | 公式HP名 = | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city | 地図2 = }} '''Myoju-in''' (みょうじゅいん, Myoju-in) is a temple, also known as '''Fushimi Kōshin-dō''' (ふしみ こうしんどう), associated with [[:en:Kōshin|Koushin Faith]]. Its mountain name is '''Kōenzan''' (こうえんざん). It is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|ja|真言宗醍醐派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism Daigo-ha|qq=}}, enshrining its principal image, the {{ill|Great Seimen Kongō|ja|大青面金剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Seimen Kongō|qq=}} (dai-shōmen kongō, also known as "Koushin-san"). This principal image is venerated for its reputed efficacy in gathering faith to treat women's illnesses, other ailments, and to promote marital harmony. == History == The temple was founded in 1638. Initially, the temple complex included the large main hall and various subsidiary halls, along with an adjacent park area to the north of the precincts. In 1698, it became the object of a petition by the 113th Emperor of Higashiyama. During the mid-Meiji period (1868–1912), the main hall and other structures were demolished, and the temple grounds were reduced to their current scale. == Address == [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|Fushimi Ward]] Masuya-cho 614 == Principal image == In the main hall, the principal image of the Great Seimen Kongō is enshrined. This hidden buddha (秘仏) is housed within a portable shrine (厨子内). It is exhibited only once every 60 years, in the year of {{ill|Koushin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koushin|qq=}} (kanoesaru). It is believed to ward off severe illnesses—excluding those severe, contagious conditions known as "denshiki" (伝死鬼). Since such diseases were thought to be transmitted by unseen insects, it was believed that defeating the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) would expel these maladies, thus becoming syncretized with Koushin. Since ancient times, ritual practices have been performed here to expel all malignant spirits and demons and to fulfill all prayers. The "Seimen Kongō Dharma" is recited daily at the temple, and an imperial regalia display (御前立) is also present. Additionally, attendant deities enshrined include four Great Yasha, two Great Child Attendants, the [[:en:Three wise monkeys|Three Monkeys]] (see no evil, speak no evil, hear no evil), a waiting attendant (脇待), a statue of [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] from the Kamakura period, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|弘法大師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, and a tablet dedicated to [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. == About Koushin == The term "Koushin" is derived from the combination of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Tenkan]] (the ten Heavenly Stems: kō, otsu, hei, tei, bo, ki, kō, shin, jin, ki) and the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Jūnishi]] (the twelve Earthly Branches: ne, ushi, tora, u, tatsu, mi, uma, hi, saru, tori, inu, i). There are 60 possible combinations, which recur once every 60 days. On the night of a Koushin day, people remain awake in an event known as "Koushin-machi" (waiting for Koushin). It is believed that the "Sanshi no Mushi" (three insects that dwell within the human body) reside therein. When people eventually fall asleep on a Koushin night, these insects are thought to crawl out of the body. The insects were believed to report the misdeeds committed by a person to the heavenly emperor, thereby determining that person's lifespan. Thus, by staying awake during Koushin-machi, people pray for protection, longevity, and the purity of body and spirit. == Amulets == === Kukkuri Saru === This amulet depicts a monkey with its arms and legs bound, rendering it immobile. The monkey symbolizes the desires that reside in the human heart. Just as a monkey may scurry about, these desires are thought to move within the heart, causing one to neglect effort, commit misdeeds, or hinder the fulfillment of one's wishes. By binding these desires, it is believed that Koushin controls them. Within the Kukkuri Saru, a talisman of the Great Seimen Kongō is housed, and through the secret technique of "kaigen" (awakening), a soul is imbued. == Annual Events == * Konnyaku Fūji Dai Kigan-kai (a rigorous konnyaku prayer ritual aimed at curing all diseases, joint pain, and lower back pain) (January 7) * Hoshi Matsuri (a ritual for good fortune and exorcism at the start of spring, featuring a great fire ritual with Shaitō Daigoma and fire-walking training) (February 11) * Spring Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Vernal Equinox) * Seirei Mukae Gane (a bell-ringing ritual during Obon) * Shōgaki Kuyō-kai (a Hungry Ghost Memorial Service) (August 16) * Autumn Equinox Midday Memorial Service (on the Autumnal Equinox) * Women's Guardian Prayer Meeting (a rigorous prayer meeting for women's happiness and protection) (3rd Sunday of October) * Emperor Higashiyama Memorial and Columbarium Mass (December 17) * Joya no Kane (bell ringing on New Year's Eve, December 31) * Monthly memorial services for ancestors and mizuko (water child) memorial services, as well as Koushin Goma (on the 7th day of every month) * Prayer meetings (on Koushin days) {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Kyoto Prefecture}} ]] }} {{translated page|ja|明壽院|version=101308058|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:ill|Temples in Fushimi Ward, Kyoto|ja|京都市伏見区の寺}} [[sn:Myoju-in]] }} [[ja:明壽院]] [[en:明壽院]] [[sn:明壽院]] [[Category:京都市伏見区の寺]] [[Category:Kōshin]]
